title: Yahoo Thunderbird Setup Failure
creator: obrendan211
content:
Attempting to set up a yahoo email in thunderbird 140.9.0esr(64-bit). The goal is to have this set up on multiple workstations, so I've made the adjustments recommended per this article: https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/thunderbird-and-yahoo#w_please-check-the-following-before-asking-for-support

So, the toggle for mailnews.oauth.usePrivateBrowser is set to true, site/third-party cookies are always allowed (no exceptions), and the authentication method is set to OAuth2. Using the recommended server settings for yahoo: imap.mail.yahoo.com : 993 smtp.mail.yahoo.com : 465

I am able to set up the initial OAuth. The window pops up, I log in using the account password then put in the texted code, then the window closes and I get an email confirming the login. Hitting done within Thunderbird after this has it check the password again, and it fails: "Unable to log in at server. Probably wrong configuration, username or password."

This is on a fresh Windows 11 install/fresh Thunderbird install.

title: New installation of Thunderbird does not find existing email accounts on local drive.
creator: Al01
content:
I just installed Thunderbird on Linux Mint (version 22.2), when I launch the application it automatically logs into my email account on my Internet access provider. I do not want that. I want the way the old version started up: it opened my local folders on my local drive, listed previously down-loaded mail and on the left panel it listed the folders on my main (current) email account followed my the list of my other email accounts/names. The top menu let me (among other actions) select to download email to the local email directory. I do not want Thunderbird to automatically log onto my web account or sync my local directory to the online email account. When I want to read my email I want to select to download the mail from the server. When I want to access the online account I log onto it through my web host's cpanel tool.

My local hard drive has a thunderbird directory (in my home directly) from the previous Thunderbird version. It contains a subdirector labeled Mail, which contains a directory list of my email addresses, in addition to one labeled Local Folders. There is another subdirectory labeled Mail.sbd.

Is there a way the new version can be set up similar to the old version. The installed Thunderbird version is 140.9.0esr (64-bit)

title: Outlook.com accounts not working in Thunderbird – intermittent OAuth2 login errors
creator: kukkulat
content:
I am using Thunderbird 149.0 (64‑bit) on Windows 11 25H2.

Recently, Thunderbird has started showing error messages for my Outlook.com accounts every time the program starts. Sign‑in fails, and sometimes an additional error appears: Error code 12. 

I have three Outlook.com accounts in Thunderbird. All of them use the IMAP server: outlook.office365.com.

When I open and close Thunderbird several times, the login eventually succeeds at random. After that, Thunderbird re-downloads all emails from the server, which suggests that the OAuth2 token is not updating correctly. All accounts are using OAuth2 authentication. One suggestion was to switch to the App Password method, because Outlook.com OAuth2 is said to be unstable in third‑party applications. Question: Is the App Password method less secure than OAuth2, and is it advisable to switch to it for Outlook.com accounts in Thunderbird or something else what i can do?

title: Thunderbird, 3 utenti ma solo 2 pass memorizzate
creator: alberto.v
content:
Buongiorno a tutti e grazie in anticipo per la vs attenzione, sono migrato da poco su thunderbird con i miei utenti di posta (3 in totale). Uno dei 3 utenti non scarica i messaggi ma mi sono accorto che se chiedo il download mi chiede la pass di accesso (al server di posta). Controllando le pass salvate sul client risultano solo 2 utenti (uno di fastweb e uno di iol) ma in realtà io ho configurato DUE utenti su IOL.... Come posso fare a memorizzare la pass del secondo utente che non risulta presente nella configurazione delle password? Non c'è un pulsante aggiungi.... In pratica.... utente pippo@fastwebnet... regolare... utente pluto@iol.it... regolare... utente papaerino@iol.it... manca la password memorizzata...

Spero di essere stato abbastanza comprensibile. Grazie a tutti per l'aiuto... Alberto

title: Clean install, no 3rd party AV, Tbird downloads a small amount of mail then gives "unable to write the email to the mailbox" error.
creator: fred.lorrain
content: Hey all, I installed the current version of Tbird on a couple of machines for a friend, one machine worked fine for a month or so, then started giving the "unable to write the email to the mailbox, make sure the file system allows you write privileges, and you have enough disk space to copy the mailbox" error. There is a ton of free space on his C: drive, hundreds of GB. Yesterday I installed Thunderbird to another of his PCs, also no 3rd party AV (both running Win 11), same exact error after downloading a handful of emails from a few weeks ago. To make things weirder he took his laptop (the machine that worked ok for a short time) to his office, where he has a different ISP and it downloaded all of his mail! What is going on here? I have seen plenty of threads about this error, I tried compacting, repairing the folder etc, nothing fixes the problem and Thunderbird sits there not syncing his mail. At the bottom of the window it shows that there are 200 or so emails to download, it looks like they are downloading for a bit, then it quits trying and gives the error. Is there a known cause of this seemingly common error? Are Thunderbird devs aware there's an issue that renders their software unuseable for some users? His mail account is with Brightspeed, an old centurytel.net account. Please share any tips you may have for overcoming this error, thanks!
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:148.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/148.0;os:Windows 10/11
